heading: Expiration date; exception
label: 1591c Expiration date; exception
text: After , no construction of permanent housing may be begun under subchapter IX of this chapter. After , (a) no mortgage may be insured under title IX of the National Housing Act, as amended [ et seq.] (except (i) pursuant to a commitment to insure issued on or before such date or (ii) after , and until , during such period, or for such project or projects, as the President may designate hereunder or (iii) pursuant to a commitment to insure issued pursuant to the preceding clause (ii)), (b) no agreement may be made to extend assistance for the provision of community facilities or services under subchapter IX of this chapter, and no construction of temporary housing or community facilities by the United States may be begun under such subchapter, except after , and until , during such period, or for such project or projects, as the President may designate hereunder: , That to the extent necessary to assure the adequate completion of any facilities for which prior agreements have been made under subchapter IX, the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development may, at any time after , enter into amendatory agreements under such subchapter involving the expenditure of additional Federal funds within the balance available therefor on or before such date, (c) no loan may be made or obligations purchased by the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development under (except pursuant to a commitment issued on or before , or to refinance an existing loan or existing obligations held under such section by said Secretary on ).June 30, 1953July 31, 1954July 31, 1954August 1, 1955July 31, 1954August 1, 1955July 31, 1954June 30, 1953June 30, 195312 U.S.C. 1750section 1701g–1 of title 12Provided
notes: References in TextThe National Housing Act, referred to in text, is , . Title IX of the National Housing Act is title IX of , as added by , , which is classified generally to subchapter X (§ 1750 et seq.) of chapter 13 of Title 12, Banks and Banking. For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see and Tables.act June 27, 1934, ch. 84748 Stat. 1246act June 27, 1934, ch. 847act Sept. 1, 1951, ch. 378, title II, § 20165 Stat. 295section 1701 of Title 12, referred to in text, has been omitted from the Code.Section 1701g–1 of title 12

Amendments1955—Act , inserted item (iii) in cl. (a).Aug. 11, 1955Act , substituted “” for “” in two places in second sentence.June 30, 1955August 1, 1955July 1, 19551954—Act , gave President standby authority to use mortgage insurance authority under title IX of the National Housing Act and the provisions in subchapter IX of this chapter for Federal aid in providing defense housing and community facilities and services in critical defense areas, in substitution for provisions under which authority for new projects under these two programs would have expired on , and authorized the Housing and Home Finance Administrator to enter into amendatory agreements after , to provide additional Federal assistance with respect to defense community facilities undertaken on or before such date where he finds it necessary to do so to assure the adequate completion of such facilities.Aug. 2, 1954June 30, 1954June 30, 1954Act , substituted “” for “” at beginning of second sentence.June 29, 1954July 31, 1954June 30, 19541953—Act , inserted sentence prohibiting the beginning of permanent housing construction under subchapter IX of this chapter, after ; substituted “” for “” at beginning of present second sentence and “temporary housing” for “housing” in cl. (b) of present second sentence; struck a former cl. (c) out of existing second sentence which provided that (after ) no land might be acquired by the Housing and Home Finance Administrator under subchapter X of this chapter; and redesignated cl. (d) as (c).June 30, 1953June 30, 1953June 30, 1954June 30, 1953June 30, 1953
Transfer of FunctionsFor transfer of functions to Secretary of Housing and Urban Development, see note set out under .section 1581 of this title
